_comment: QueryStudio configuration. All values can be overridden by environment variables (see config.py).

paths._comment: Relative paths are resolved from the backend/ directory. Use absolute paths for OpenShift/container deployments.

database._comment_postgresql: Set type=postgresql and postgresql_url (or QUERYSTUDIO_DATABASE_POSTGRESQL_URL env var). Falls back to SQLite if PG is unreachable.
database._comment_oracle: Set type=oracle and fill oracle_* fields (or use QUERYSTUDIO_DATABASE_* env vars) to use Oracle as the metadata store instead of SQLite.

auth._comment: User authentication. type='none' (default, no login) | 'local' (config-based users) | 'ad' (Active Directory / LDAP).
auth._comment_local_users: Format: user1:pass1,user2:pass2. Only used when type=local.
auth._comment_user_roles: Format: user1:admin,user2:analyst,user3:viewer. Overrides AD group-based role.
auth._comment_ad_use_ssl: Set true for ldaps:// connections (port 636). Auto-detected from the ldaps:// URL scheme.
auth._comment_ad_tls_validate: Validate the AD server's TLS certificate. Set false for self-signed certs in dev (Option A quick unblock). Leave true in prod + set ad_tls_ca_file.
auth._comment_ad_tls_check_hostname: Verify cert CN/SAN matches ad_server hostname. Default false because AD certs are issued to the DC FQDN (e.g. dc01.corp.com), not the domain name (corp.com). Set true only if your cert has SANs matching ad_server.
auth._comment_ad_tls_ca_file: Absolute path to a PEM CA bundle for the AD server cert. Empty = use the OS default trust store. In OpenShift, mount a Secret at /app/certs/ad-ca/ca.crt and set this to that path.
auth._comment_ad: Set type=ad and fill ad_* fields (or QUERYSTUDIO_AUTH_* env vars). Groups are comma-separated AD group names mapped to roles.

scheduler._comment: Automated cleanup intervals and retention windows.
scheduler._comment_cleanup: Delete unregistered temp result files older than N hours (runs every 30 min). Default: 1
scheduler._comment_retention: Delete query execution history + result parquet files older than N days (runs daily). Default: 30

storage._comment: Disk space management for the downloads folder.
storage._comment_expiry: How many days a Download Centre file stays 'available' before expiring. Default: 7
storage._comment_quota: Soft quota for the downloads folder in MB. Oldest files are expired first when exceeded. Default: 10240 (10 GB)
storage._comment_cache: Max size of the query result cache in MB before LRU eviction. Default: 2048 (2 GB)

cors._comment: For production, replace * with your actual domain: https://querystudio.example.com

cache._comment: Query result cache â€” served from disk parquet, zero extra servers required.

email._comment: SMTP settings for schedule notifications. Leave host empty to disable email.
email._comment_env: Override with QUERYSTUDIO_EMAIL_HOST, QUERYSTUDIO_EMAIL_PORT, QUERYSTUDIO_EMAIL_USER, QUERYSTUDIO_EMAIL_PASSWORD, QUERYSTUDIO_EMAIL_FROM_ADDRESS, QUERYSTUDIO_EMAIL_USE_TLS

chatbot._comment: Column classification patterns for the AI Assistant chip suggestions.
chatbot._comment_usage: Patterns are matched case-insensitively as substrings of column names. Add your organisation-specific column names or partial keywords to extend auto-detection.
chatbot._comment_numeric: Columns where SUM / AVG / MIN / MAX are meaningful.
chatbot._comment_category: Columns used for GROUP BY, COUNT BY, or DISTINCT analysis.
chatbot._comment_date: Columns used for trend / time-series analysis.
chatbot._comment_id: ID / key columns â€” SUM/AVG not meaningful, only COUNT/FILTER.

faq._comment: Keyword â†’ answer map for the AI Assistant. Keys are matched case-insensitively as substrings of the user's message. Add organisation-specific Q&A here.
faq.what is querystudio: QueryStudio is a self-service data query and analytics platform. It lets you query databases, S3 files, and uploaded data â€” then visualise results as charts, pivot tables, and dashboards.
faq.who built querystudio: QueryStudio is built in-house as an internal analytics platform.
faq.contact support: Raise a support ticket with your IT / Data Engineering team, or email the platform owner.
faq.what data sources: QueryStudio connects to: Amazon S3 (Parquet/CSV/JSON), Snowflake, Databricks, Trino, Oracle, and local file uploads (CSV, Excel, Parquet, JSON).
faq.what file formats: Supported upload formats: CSV, Excel (.xlsx), Parquet, JSON. Results can be downloaded as CSV, Excel, or Parquet.
faq.how many rows: The default query limit is 1,000 rows. Admins can raise this per role. Use streaming mode for large datasets.
faq.data retention: Query execution history is kept for 30 days by default. Download files expire after 7 days.
faq.is my data secure: QueryStudio runs within your organisation's network. Credentials are encrypted, queries run server-side, and role-based access controls limit what each user can see.

glossary._comment: Business term â†’ technical column/table/SQL fragment. Applied automatically when NL-to-SQL runs. Teach the chatbot your org's data vocabulary.
glossary._comment_examples: Map a business word to an actual column name, table name, or SQL snippet. E.g. 'revenue': 'sale_amount' means 'show revenue' becomes 'show sale_amount'.

suggestion_corrections._comment: Smart aggregation and join correction learning. The system learns from user overrides.

partition_cache._comment: Two-tier partition cache: L1 in-memory (fast) + L2 SQLite (survives restarts). TTLs in seconds.

report_catalog._comment: Phase 58: Unified report catalog with certification, sharing, and embedding.

business_portal._comment: Phase 59: Business Portal landing page settings.
